name: Elect Barbara Lee via Our Revolution
subject: Early vote is underway for Barbara Lee: the Peace candidate in the race for U.S. Senate in California
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/14274?t=1011?refcode=email-20240211&akid=5322%2E5885482%2EnZKxrR ]Our Revolution logo

Peter Willis,

Early voting is underway in California and Our Revolution is mobilizing on
behalf of Barbara Lee because she is the movement candidate in this race.
She’s the Peace candidate in this race. And she’s the true progressive who
we can elect to join Bernie in the Senate.

Our Revolution phone bankers and organizers have already made 1 million
voter contacts for Barbara Lee and we want to make another million before
primary voting ends on March 5th.

Barbara is up against corporate-funded opponents, and she’ll need every
single bit of GOTV, phone banking, and door knocking behind her so she can
join Bernie in the Senate. Organized people can beat organized money.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/14274?t=1048?express_lane=true&amount=27&refcode=email-20240211&akid=5322%2E5885482%2EnZKxrR ]Will you donate now to help us make one million calls to elect Barbara Lee
to the U.S. Senate. Every dollar counts right now as we contact voters
with ballots in their hands every single day.

If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation to Our Revolution will process immediately.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/14274?t=1035?express_lane=true&amount=12&refcode=email-20240211&akid=5322%2E5885482%2EnZKxrR ]CHIP IN $12 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/14274?t=1036?express_lane=true&amount=27&refcode=email-20240211&akid=5322%2E5885482%2EnZKxrR ]CHIP IN $27
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/14274?t=1037?express_lane=true&amount=35&refcode=email-20240211&akid=5322%2E5885482%2EnZKxrR ]CHIP IN $35 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/14274?t=1038?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=email-20240211&akid=5322%2E5885482%2EnZKxrR ]CHIP IN $50
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/14274?t=1039?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=email-20240211&akid=5322%2E5885482%2EnZKxrR ]CHIP IN $100 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/14274?t=1040?&amount=12&refcode=email-20240211&akid=5322%2E5885482%2EnZKxrR ]CHIP IN OTHER AMOUNT

We’re joining Barbara for a big rally in San Francisco next weekend and
then going out to knock doors to make sure voters fill in their ballots
for the one movement candidate, Peace candidate, and true progressive in
the race.

Barbara’s the only candidate who supported a Ceasefire in Israel-Palestine
since day one of Israel’s bombing campaign in Gaza. She’s been a singular
courageous voice for Peace in Congress for decades — she was the lone vote
against the war in Afghanistan in 2001.

She was an original cosponsor of Medicare for All and the first to
introduce a single payer healthcare bill in CA — and she’s long been a
staunch champion for climate, education, housing, wages, labor rights, and
more.

Barbara Lee has been a constant voice for people, peace, and the planet
throughout her career — and we need her in the Senate. [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/14579?t=1052&akid=5322%2E5885482%2EnZKxrR ]But we can only
have the kind of GOTV campaign we need to elect her with your support.

We’re not only phone banking, door knocking, and texting progressives
throughout California, we’re also executing an effective online
advertising campaign in support of Barbara — and political ads are
expensive, Peter Willis.

Our Revolution has been on the ground with Barbara Lee since early on in
this campaign, strategizing with the team, and working with local
progressive leaders and grassroots activists to help get the word out
about Barbara as the clear progressive choice to become California's next
U.S. Senator.

We need Barbara Lee to join Bernie Sanders in the U.S. Senate and build a
progressive bloc in the Senate. And we can only continue this work with
your support.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/14274?t=1046?express_lane=true&amount=27&refcode=email-20240211&akid=5322%2E5885482%2EnZKxrR ]Contribute now to elect Barbara Lee — another unwavering progressive to
join Bernie Sanders in the Senate!

When we organize, we win.

Our Revolution

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/9?t=1001&akid=5322%2E5885482%2EnZKxrR ]Facebook | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/10?t=1002&akid=5322%2E5885482%2EnZKxrR ]Twitter | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/11?t=1003&akid=5322%2E5885482%2EnZKxrR ]Instagram | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/2355?t=1004&akid=5322%2E5885482%2EnZKxrR ]Youtube | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/2356?t=1005&akid=5322%2E5885482%2EnZKxrR ]Website |

Our Revolution is the largest entirely grassroots funded progressive
group in America.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/13135?t=1008&akid=5322%2E5885482%2EnZKxrR ]Help us defend democracy and take the fight directly to the
establishment by committing to be a 2024 Movement Builder with a
recurring donation of just $5 or more a month!

name: Team Spanberger
subject: We’ll keep this short
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Governor
body:
A successful fundraising month not only helps put together a winning campaign but also has a deeper meaning for this campaign.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/78826607/454580999/1993432125?refcode=BLU_EM_ASFG_2024-02-11_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNzIwZDVhZmUtMTFjOS1lZTExLTg1ZjktMDAyMjQ4MjIzNzk0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Xq7bQ4wi5Ete9AJAe_SrjMSX5C277WlKzHLyBQcDqKk=)
---
| Peter,

We’ll keep this short –

February is the shortest month of the year, and this year, even with one extra day, we only have 29 days to hit our fundraising goal!

That’s why we’re doing everything we can to meet our goals before the end of the month sneaks up on us.

A successful fundraising month not only helps put together a winning campaign but also has a deeper meaning for this campaign. It’s confirmation that we have a devoted coalition of supporters in our corner.

Abigail's campaigns have always been built on strong grassroots support, and this time around, it’s even more important. name: Alsobrooks HQ
subject: Team Alsobrooks Newsletter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alsobrooks for Senate
body:
|
---
[ ](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/2092?t=1&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu)

| | Greetings #TeamAlsobrooks, We had another great week on the campaign trail, thank you to everyone who showed up and showed out for us! Our growing support across the state is truly inspiring. Having the privilege to speak with people across Maryland and to envision a better future for our great state is truly remarkable! Take a look below for some highlights from the campaign trail and beyond! We had a huge crowd of 150 people in Howard County and discussed issues ranging from gun safety to reproductive rights to improving schools for our kids. Special thanks to Howard County Executive Calvin Ball for gathering such an outstanding crowd! [](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/10550?t=21&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu) [](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/10550?t=22&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu) In College Park, we had the pleasure of speaking with some UMD students about issues they care about most. They shared their opinions and concerns on diversity in Congress and how it reflects our country, the cost of living and education, and the engagement of youth in politics. Students are dialed in to what’s happening across the country and they’re ready to vote in 2024! [](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/10550?t=23&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu) [](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/10550?t=24&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu) What a glorious Sunday in Baltimore! Thank you to Bishop Dwayne Debnam for the powerful sermon and for inviting us to Morning Star Baptist Church. Always great to see our friend, Baltimore County Executive Johnny Olszewski. Another thank you to Bishop Donté L. Hickman for welcoming us to the Southern Baptist Church for their special Black History Month service. What a compelling reminder of the community’s strength and resilience that continues to propel in order to achieve true equity. [](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/10550?t=25&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu) [](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/10550?t=26&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu) We met with local business owners at The Breakfast Club in Silver Spring, where we had a fascinating discussion about the importance of investing in small businesses throughout our state. Let’s fight to make sure Marylanders in every zip code have the chance at economic prosperity! [](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/10550?t=27&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu) Thank you to Travis Bell for the hospitality you shared at Black Acres Roastery, a coffee shop in Baltimore that not only makes the most amazing coffee and pastries (still thinking about that almond croissant), but also continues to innovate the coffee industry with their own brand of coffee beans. Small businesses, like Black Acres Roastery, are the backbone of our local economies. By providing crucial access to capital, we can ensure that entrepreneurs of all backgrounds thrive. [](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/10550?t=28&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu) We had a wonderful conversation with the Silver Angels senior group in East Baltimore about how to lower the costs of prescription drugs and how we can make healthcare more accessible for seniors. It’s vital that we do our part to support our elders, so they can enjoy a happy and healthy retirement. [](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/10550?t=19&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu) As you can see, we had a jam-packed week with crowded events. We’re proud to be the only grassroots campaign in this election. We are powered by the people, by the many. That’s how we win this race. In May and November.
### **NEWS OF THE WEEK**
**NATIONAL PROFILE:**[Maryland Senate candidate Angela Alsobrooks wants to boost 'first chances' so the economy can thrive. She'd also like to see the filibuster gone.](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/10510?t=7&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu) **_“Maryland Senate candidate Angela Alsobrooks stressed the importance of ‘first chances’ in boosting societal outcomes. ‘There is no economic growth without us investing in humans,’ Alsobrooks told BI in an interview.”_** **_[National Organization for Women Political Action Committee endorses Angela Alsobrooks](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/10511?t=8&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu)_** **_“I am thrilled to receive an endorsement from NOW PAC. I am committed to being a fierce advocate for women in the Senate...”_** **_[Angela Alsobrooks on the Dean Obeidallah Show on Sirius XM](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/10512?t=9&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu)_** **_“Alsobrooks told Dean Obeidallah that if elected to the Senate, she will fight to protect the rights Republicans are trying to take away.”_** [**_Prince George’s County Exec. Alsobrooks files paperwork to run for US Senate_**](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/10547?t=10&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu) **_“Prince George’s County Executive Angela Alsobrooks on Thursday filed the paperwork with the State Board of Elections in her run for U.S. Senate.”_** **[7News sits down with Prince George's County Executive Angela Alsobrooks](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/10514?t=11&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu)** _“From local first responders to the county budget to better serve the community, 7News ... joins Prince George's County Executive to talk about your community, once again.”_
### CAMPAIGN UPDATES
We’re extremely excited to announce that we’re accepting applications for our Fellowship Program aimed at high school and college students. If you know a young person looking to get some hands-on experience in politics, encourage them to apply [**here**](http://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/9723?t=12&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu). Here on Team Alsobrooks, we’re proud to be a campaign powered by grassroots donors. We believe in Angela's ability to go further and do better for our communities, and we know you do too! It’s going to take all of us working together to get Angela over the finish line this November. | | | [Volunteer](https://alsobrooks.actionkit.com/signup/volunteer-form?t=13&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu)
---
| | [Donate](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/10550?t=20&amount=25&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu)
---
### **SOCIALS**
While we'll keep sending emails to keep you updated, the best way to keep up with Angela is on our social media channels. Follow us on Twitter, Facebook, and Instagram to follow along with us on the campaign trail!
| | [Facebook](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/8814?t=14&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu)
---
| | [Twitter](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/8815?t=15&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu)
---
| | [Instagram](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/8816?t=16&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu)
---
Sincerely,
Team Alsobrooks

| | [ ](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/89?t=2&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu)
---

| | We believe that emails are the best way for our organization to stay in touch with supporters like you. But if you'd like to receive fewer emails, [click here.](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu)
---
[ ](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/115?t=4&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu)
| | [ Chip in a one-time donation ](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/4693?t=5&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu)
---
| | [ Chip in a monthly recurring donation! ](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/4694?t=6&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu)
---

| If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please click [here to unsubscribe](https://alsobrooks.actionkit.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=17&akid=1055%2E52803%2EEYhmIu).
| Paid for by Alsobrooks for Senate
---
Alsobrooks for Senate
PO Box 6058
Largo, MD 20792

name: Officer Harry Dunn (ret.)
subject: January 6th, 2021
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Seth Moulton for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/m24-dunn [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/m24-dunn]
Hey – my name is Harry Dunn and three years ago, I defended the U.S. Capitol and our nation’s leaders against a violent insurrection.
Since that day, I vowed to help ensure that never happens again.
That’s why I’m officially running for Congress in MD-03 to be the change I want to see for our country and our democracy. I’d be honored for you to join me and Seth Moulton in jump starting this campaign by chipping in a split donation of $25 or anything you can: [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/m24-dunn]
BECOME A FOUNDING DONOR: CHIP IN $25 >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/m24-dunn]
For the last sixteen years, I was a police officer for the United States Capitol where I saw millions of people visit to exercise their rights and lobby their representatives.
And trust me, every political agenda you can think of walked through those doors.
It didn’t matter to me if you were there as a Democrat, Republican, or Independent though, because my job was the same - protect our elected officials and ensure everyone could safely participate in our democracy.
But three years ago, under the Capitol Rotunda that took my breath away each day, I was immersed in a profane mix of sweat, screams, shrieks, anger, death, fear, blood, broken limbs, spit, hatred, horror, racism, bigotry, and heroism for what felt like endless hours alongside fellow officers.
It was unlike anything we had seen before. Sure, there were heated political moments over the years. But this was different.
It was pepper spray, bear spray, and even WD-40 to our faces as we fought back rioters in tactical gear using anything they could as a weapon. It was disorienting chaos as we tried to block every entrance while our elected leaders and capitol staff ran to safety. It was a direct attack on our democracy.
Many people have tried to deny what happened that day – including some of the same people who stood behind us as we protected them. They’ve diluted the story and muddied the facts to preserve their own political agenda.
So I’ve made it a personal mission to share my story, speak truth to power, and work to ensure this never happens again. That’s why a few weeks ago, I retired from the police force, and why I announced my campaign to run for Congress in Maryland’s Third Congressional District.
I’m running because Maryland deserves a Congressman who will put the country above their party, and put the constitution above themselves. This will be a competitive race, so I’ve set an ambitious goal to raise $500,000 within the first week of my campaign. Will you split a donation between my campaign and Seth Moulton to become a Founding Donor and help hit our goal before midnight? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/m24-dunn]
BECOME A FOUNDING DONOR: CHIP IN $25 >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/m24-dunn]
Thank you for reading,
Harry Dunn
Donate [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/m24-dunn]

subject: A game-changing campaign
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
Now, we’re gearing up to run a game-changing campaign on all fronts.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20240211-3495-footer&t=1&akid=3495%2E420207%2ExrYEwi)
Peter,
In January, we worked harder than ever to prepare for what's already becoming a competitive election year.
**And now, we’re gearing up to run a game-changing campaign on all fronts.** We’re covering all the bases, from field organizing that reaches neighborhoods across the country to a digital campaign that builds support for our work, in every battleground state.
**2024 is going to be a tide-turning year for our progressive movement,****but only if we work together.**[**Can I count on you to chip in $3 today to boost our efforts?**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20240211.uaj3WV&refcode=em20240211-3495&t=2&akid=3495%2E420207%2ExrYEwi)
It’s clear: _the system does not work for us_ _, Peter._ So we need to be prepared to challenge chaotic Republicans, big corporate power, massive super PACs, and an _entire_ system created to make the rich richer and the poor poorer.
This year we have a momentous opportunity to undo this broken status quo and legislate with compassion and common sense. [**If you’re ready to see real change in Congress and in our communities, chip in today to help us keep up this fight for progress.**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20240211.uaj3WV&refcode=em20240211-3495&t=3&akid=3495%2E420207%2ExrYEwi)
**[Contribute](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20240211.uaj3WV&refcode=em20240211-3495&t=22&akid=3495%2E420207%2ExrYEwi)**
Together,
Pramila Jayapal
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20240211-3495-footer&t=23&refcode2=3495_420207_xrYEwi&akid=3495%2E420207%2ExrYEwi)**
---
[](https://www.facebook.com/pramila.jayapal) | [](https://twitter.com/pramilajayapal) | [](https://www.instagram.com/pramilajayapal/)
Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/did-your-info-change/?t=24&akid=3495%2E420207%2ExrYEwi)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=25&akid=3495%2E420207%2ExrYEwi)

Paid for by Pramila for Congress

name: The Office of Delegate Briana D. Sewell
subject: Del. Sewell's Week 5 Session Newsletter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Briana Sewell
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87444/images/New%20Sewell%20Delegate%20Logo%20.png]
Session Week 5 Newsletter
Hello neighbors,
What a week here in the General Assembly as we inch closer and closer to crossover. We are all working to ensure our bills are passed and ready for February 13th, that time where bills passed by the House of Delegates prior to midnight are sent to the Senate for consideration, and vice versa. Any bills not passed by their respective body by crossover will not get a chance to become law during the 2024 General Assembly session.
Here is a helpful explanation of crossover from last year [https://vadogwood.com/2023/02/07/heres-a-breakdown-of-crossover-how-it-works-and-why-it-matters/] from the Virginia-based newsroom, the Dogwood.
The crossover process highlights the differences between the two chambers of the Virginia General Assembly, because so often, the disagreements on policies during crossover aren’t only due to party affiliation, but also due to disagreements between Delegates and Senators. The House and the Senate have different cultures; the House has more members and is often considered to be the more progressive of the two, while the Senate is much smaller and has historically been considered more moderate, although that assumption will be tested this year with a large number of new members in both chambers.
These differences are baked into the entire process, and we know that we won’t see eye to eye on everything. The Democratic majorities in both chambers understand that we need to deliver for the people of Virginia, that we were elected to take action, and we remain unified in that effort.
Delivering For Veterans in the 25th District
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87444/images/12.png]
Members of the VFW join Delegate Sewell to lobby for HB736, The Veterans Services Protection Act
As someone who represents a community in Prince William County where 1 in 6 residents are veterans, I am proud to have introduced HB736 [https://lis.virginia.gov/cgi-bin/legp604.exe?241+ful+HB736+hil] , the Veterans' Services Protection Act. This bill updates the existing Virginia Consumer Protection Act to crack down on claim sharks . Claim sharks are individuals or companies that charge hefty fees to assist or consult with veterans who are filing their claims with the Veterans Benefits Administration without undergoing an federal accreditation process.
In 2022, President Biden signed the PACT Act [https://www.va.gov/disability/eligibility/hazardous-materials-exposure/specific-environmental-hazards/] into law. The act greatly expanded [https://virginiamercury.com/2023/08/07/veterans-exposed-to-burn-pits-toxins-urged-to-apply-for-retroactive-benefits/] the number of veterans who could claim benefits from the VA. Specifically, it provided for veterans who were exposed to toxic chemicals and other environmental hazards to seek care and coverage. While the act represented a big win for veterans and their families, it also increased the number of predatory individuals eager to take advantage of veterans seeking help.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87444/images/WisemanKenCOAVAwithcover300.jpg] "When veterans come home from military service, they need to know that the benefits they earned for serving their country will not make them the target of a new enemy. Predatory companies operating outside of current federal rules governing people and companies who prepare claims for these benefits must be stopped. The Veterans of Foreign Wars is proud to support Delegate Sewell's bipartisan bill, HB736, which will force these Claim Sharks to follow the already existing federal regulations and ensure that veterans and their families are safe in the Commonwealth."
Ken Wiseman
Past State Commander
VFW Department of Virginia
Because these individuals and organizations are not accredited by the Virginia Department of Veterans Services or US Department of Veterans Affairs, they aren’t required to follow any of the established professional and ethical standards by the VA or abide by guardrails like background checks and continuing education requirements required of accredited claims support businesses.
These non-accredited businesses take advantage of veterans by giving misleading, incorrect, and fraudulent advice in pursuit of a higher profit at all costs. These business practices have wide ranging impacts, from impeding veterans’ ability to get the benefits they deserve to causing serious economic harm to the veterans they claim to serve.
To stop these individuals and organizations from preying on our veterans, my bill outright bans non-accredited organizations from receiving compensation for preparing veterans’ claims, as well as referring veterans or their families to banned entities. The bill also protects those representatives and attorneys who are accredited, trustworthy, and legally allowed to handle claims.
This bill passed the House of Delegates this week, and is on its way to the Senate. It will “crossover!” I am so honored to have worked with veterans groups, advocates, family members and others who helped get this bill to where it is and for sharing their stories. As the daughter of two veterans, this issue is extremely personal. It is our duty to look out for the men and women who looked out for us, and that means standing up for our veterans, protecting them from exploitation, and ensuring their access to high-quality support, resources, and care.
Fighting for a Fairer and More Just Virginia
As bills like my Veterans’ Services Protection Act move through the House of Delegates in preparation for crossing over to the Senate for consideration, you may wonder what else the new Democratic majority in the General Assembly has been focused on during this session. What issues are we fighting for?
So much of it comes down to fairness and justice. During my time in the House of Delegates, I’ve focused on serving working families and protecting our veterans. Likewise, our Democratic majority has championed and passed critical bills that help make the Commonwealth a fairer and more just place to call home.
Whether it’s HB1 [https://lis.virginia.gov/cgi-bin/legp604.exe?ses=241&typ=bil&val=HB1] , which would increase the minimum wage to $15 for our workers, HB2 [https://lis.virginia.gov/cgi-bin/legp604.exe?ses=241&typ=bil&val=HB2] , which would make our communities safer by banning assault-style weapons, HJ1 [https://lis.virginia.gov/cgi-bin/legp604.exe?241+sum+HJ1] , which would amend the state constitution to protect the fundamental right to reproductive freedom, or HJ2 [https://lis.virginia.gov/cgi-bin/legp604.exe?241+sum+HJ2] , another constitutional amendment that would automatically restore the right to vote for hundreds of Virginians and further protect access to the ballot box, Democrats this session have focused on fighting for a fairer and more just Virginia.
These are the critical issues we’re discussing, debating, fighting for, and passing. And that united effort will continue as we head into crossover next week.
Welcome to Richmond! This Week’s Visits, Meetings & More
I want to thank everyone who visited us for taking the time to visit my office and advocate on behalf of important issues and critical pieces of legislation. This week, my team and I met with a wide variety of groups, including those below.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87444/images/Screenshot%202024-02-09%20at%204.52.27%20PM.png] I was grateful for the community members representing the Jewish Community Relations [https://jcouncil.org/] Council [https://jcouncil.org/] for sharing their stories with me this week and advocating for anti-hate crime legislation.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87444/images/Screenshot%202024-02-09%20at%204.53.47%20PM.png] I was also thrilled to meet with the incredible students of the Civil Air Patrol [https://vawg.cap.gov/] program, discussing their passion for aviation and leadership. I was so inspired by their dedication and commitment to service!
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87444/images/Screenshot%202024-02-09%20at%204.52.51%20PM.png] I welcomed members of the Realtor Association of Prince William [https://www.pwar.com/] and heard about their efforts around housing issues and the need for more affordable, accessible housing.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87444/images/Screenshot%202024-02-09%20at%204.54.10%20PM.png] I was also able to meet with representatives from the Prince William-Fauquier Area League of Women Voters [https://www.princewilliamlwv.org/] to discuss their ongoing voter initiatives and efforts to protect access to the ballot box.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87444/images/Screenshot%202024-02-09%20at%205.17.18%20PM.png] It was great to have individuals join us from the ARC of Nova [https://thearcofnova.org/] this week, discussing their advocacy on behalf of people with intellectual and developmental disabilities and how the state can support their work.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87444/images/Screenshot%202024-02-09%20at%205.16.44%20PM.png] I also had a blast meeting with members of the James Madison University Student Government this week, discussing higher education issues and getting to know the next generation of Delegates and Senators!
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87444/images/Screenshot%202024-02-09%20at%204.53.26%20PM.png] I was honored to meet with the Prince Willam County Chapter of the National Coalition of the 100 Black Women, Inc. [https://ncbwpwcc.org/] and take part in a meaningful discussion about how we can work together to empower and uplift Black women and girls, especially in the areas of education, health, economic empowerment, and public advocacy.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87444/images/THIS%20ONE.jpeg] I have had the opportunity to meet with many leaders in the higher education space this session, and as the Chair of the Higher Education Subcommittee in the House, their input and ideas have proven both helpful and insightful. That’s one of the reasons I was excited to meet with Dr. Willis Lonzer, the General President of the Alpha Phi Alpha, Inc. Fraternity [https://apa1906.net/general-president-elect/] . President Lonzer and I were were able to have an important conversation about empowering young Black men through the fraternity experience, higher education affordability, access issues, and much more.
My Bills
This week, I am happy to report that three more of my bills were voted out of committee and sent to the House floor. These bills address critical issues that are so important to Virginia’s families, like education and teachers, early childhood care for military families, and taking steps to combat the opioid epidemic:
HB 732 [https://lis.virginia.gov/cgi-bin/legp604.exe?ses=241&typ=bil&val=HB732] : My bill that would require schools to stock and train staff on the administration of Naloxone, a life-saving medication, in case of an opioid overdose. This bill will help schools be prepared to recognize and respond to the tragic extent of the opioid epidemic and give our school staff the tools and training they need to save student and staff lives. This bill passed out of the House Appropriations Committee unanimously with amendments [https://lis.virginia.gov/cgi-bin/legp604.exe?241+vot+H02V0107+HB0732] , 22-0, and is now on second read on the House Floor.
HB 739 [https://lis.virginia.gov/cgi-bin/legp604.exe?ses=241&typ=bil&val=HB739] : My bill that would exempt childcare and early education programs that serve only military families and are licensed by the DOD from unnecessary and duplicative licensure in Virginia which limits the supply of child care options for our military families. Del. Tata’s HB146 [https://lis.virginia.gov/cgi-bin/legp604.exe?241+sum+HB146] [https://lis.virginia.gov/cgi-bin/legp604.exe?241+sum+HB146] was incorporated into my bill. HB 739 passed the House Floor on Friday with a vote of 96 to 0.
HB731 [https://lis.virginia.gov/cgi-bin/legp604.exe?ses=241&typ=bil&val=HB731] : My bill that works to fix the leaky teacher pipeline and increase teacher diversity through the elimination of a duplicative, expensive, and out-of-date testing requirement for teachers. This bill will help fill teacher vacancies by ensuring this obsolete testing requirement does not push prospective teachers out of high-quality teacher preparation pathways in favor of other fields. This bill passed out of the House Education Committee with a substitute [https://lis.virginia.gov/cgi-bin/legp604.exe?ses=241&typ=bil&val=HB731] and a unanimous vote of, 22-0, and is now on second read on the House Floor.
Subcommittees
Additionally, I continue to chair three House subcommittees, and this week, two of those subcommittees met to consider the last few bills on our docket prior to crossover next week:
Subcommittee on Higher Education

This Week’s Mailbag
As the session continues, so does your mail! Your ongoing engagement is so heartening, and I appreciate everyone who takes the time to write to my team about specific bills and policies that mean the most to them.
This week, we received a lot of emails about two specific concerns: transportation issues and bills related to firearm safety and gun violence prevention.
Thank you for reading these weekly messages, for sharing your feedback and making your voice heard. Please do not hesitate to reach out throughout the ongoing General Assembly session.
Stay In Touch!
As I’ve previously mentioned, crossover begins next week on February 13th. As the House of Delegates begins considering legislation coming over from the Senate, I encourage you to stay in contact with my team and continue to provide your feedback and ideas.

clean_body: [] Session Week 5 Newsletter Hello neighbors, What a week here in the General Assembly as we inch closer and closer to crossover. We are all working to ensure our bills are passed and ready for February 13th, that time where bills passed by the House of Delegates prior to midnight are sent to the Senate for consideration, and vice versa. Any bills not passed by their respective body by crossover will not get a chance to become law during the 2024 General Assembly session. Here is a helpful explanation of crossover from last year [] from the Virginia-based newsroom, the Dogwood. The crossover process highlights the differences between the two chambers of the Virginia General Assembly, because so often, the disagreements on policies during crossover aren’t only due to party affiliation, but also due to disagreements between Delegates and Senators. The House and the Senate have different cultures; the House has more members and is often considered to be the more progressive of the two, while the Senate is much smaller and has historically been considered more moderate, although that assumption will be tested this year with a large number of new members in both chambers. These differences are baked into the entire process, and we know that we won’t see eye to eye on everything. The Democratic majorities in both chambers understand that we need to deliver for the people of Virginia, that we were elected to take action, and we remain unified in that effort. Delivering For Veterans in the 25th District [] Members of the VFW join Delegate Sewell to lobby for HB736, The Veterans Services Protection Act As someone who represents a community in Prince William County where 1 in 6 residents are veterans, I am proud to have introduced HB736 [] , the Veterans' Services Protection Act. This bill updates the existing Virginia Consumer Protection Act to crack down on claim sharks . Claim sharks are individuals or companies that charge hefty fees to assist or consult with veterans who are filing their claims with the Veterans Benefits Administration without undergoing an federal accreditation process. In 2022, President Biden signed the PACT Act [] into law. The act greatly expanded [] the number of veterans who could claim benefits from the VA. Specifically, it provided for veterans who were exposed to toxic chemicals and other environmental hazards to seek care and coverage. While the act represented a big win for veterans and their families, it also increased the number of predatory individuals eager to take advantage of veterans seeking help. [] "When veterans come home from military service, they need to know that the benefits they earned for serving their country will not make them the target of a new enemy. Predatory companies operating outside of current federal rules governing people and companies who prepare claims for these benefits must be stopped. The Veterans of Foreign Wars is proud to support Delegate Sewell's bipartisan bill, HB736, which will force these Claim Sharks to follow the already existing federal regulations and ensure that veterans and their families are safe in the Commonwealth." Ken Wiseman Past State Commander VFW Department of Virginia Because these individuals and organizations are not accredited by the Virginia Department of Veterans Services or US Department of Veterans Affairs, they aren’t required to follow any of the established professional and ethical standards by the VA or abide by guardrails like background checks and continuing education requirements required of accredited claims support businesses. These non-accredited businesses take advantage of veterans by giving misleading, incorrect, and fraudulent advice in pursuit of a higher profit at all costs. These business practices have wide ranging impacts, from impeding veterans’ ability to get the benefits they deserve to causing serious economic harm to the veterans they claim to serve. To stop these individuals and organizations from preying on our veterans, my bill outright bans non-accredited organizations from receiving compensation for preparing veterans’ claims, as well as referring veterans or their families to banned entities. The bill also protects those representatives and attorneys who are accredited, trustworthy, and legally allowed to handle claims. This bill passed the House of Delegates this week, and is on its way to the Senate. It will “crossover!” I am so honored to have worked with veterans groups, advocates, family members and others who helped get this bill to where it is and for sharing their stories. As the daughter of two veterans, this issue is extremely personal. It is our duty to look out for the men and women who looked out for us, and that means standing up for our veterans, protecting them from exploitation, and ensuring their access to high-quality support, resources, and care. Fighting for a Fairer and More Just Virginia As bills like my Veterans’ Services Protection Act move through the House of Delegates in preparation for crossing over to the Senate for consideration, you may wonder what else the new Democratic majority in the General Assembly has been focused on during this session. What issues are we fighting for? So much of it comes down to fairness and justice. During my time in the House of Delegates, I’ve focused on serving working families and protecting our veterans. Likewise, our Democratic majority has championed and passed critical bills that help make the Commonwealth a fairer and more just place to call home. Whether it’s HB1 [] , which would increase the minimum wage to $15 for our workers, HB2 [] , which would make our communities safer by banning assault-style weapons, HJ1 [] , which would amend the state constitution to protect the fundamental right to reproductive freedom, or HJ2 [] , another constitutional amendment that would automatically restore the right to vote for hundreds of Virginians and further protect access to the ballot box, Democrats this session have focused on fighting for a fairer and more just Virginia. These are the critical issues we’re discussing, debating, fighting for, and passing. And that united effort will continue as we head into crossover next week. Welcome to Richmond! This Week’s Visits, Meetings & More I want to thank everyone who visited us for taking the time to visit my office and advocate on behalf of important issues and critical pieces of legislation. This week, my team and I met with a wide variety of groups, including those below. [] I was grateful for the community members representing the Jewish Community Relations [] Council [] for sharing their stories with me this week and advocating for anti-hate crime legislation. [] I was also thrilled to meet with the incredible students of the Civil Air Patrol [] program, discussing their passion for aviation and leadership. I was so inspired by their dedication and commitment to service! [] I welcomed members of the Realtor Association of Prince William [] and heard about their efforts around housing issues and the need for more affordable, accessible housing. [] I was also able to meet with representatives from the Prince William-Fauquier Area League of Women Voters [] to discuss their ongoing voter initiatives and efforts to protect access to the ballot box. [] It was great to have individuals join us from the ARC of Nova [] this week, discussing their advocacy on behalf of people with intellectual and developmental disabilities and how the state can support their work. [] I also had a blast meeting with members of the James Madison University Student Government this week, discussing higher education issues and getting to know the next generation of Delegates and Senators! [] I was honored to meet with the Prince Willam County Chapter of the National Coalition of the 100 Black Women, Inc. [] and take part in a meaningful discussion about how we can work together to empower and uplift Black women and girls, especially in the areas of education, health, economic empowerment, and public advocacy. [] I have had the opportunity to meet with many leaders in the higher education space this session, and as the Chair of the Higher Education Subcommittee in the House, their input and ideas have proven both helpful and insightful. That’s one of the reasons I was excited to meet with Dr. Willis Lonzer, the General President of the Alpha Phi Alpha, Inc. Fraternity [] . President Lonzer and I were were able to have an important conversation about empowering young Black men through the fraternity experience, higher education affordability, access issues, and much more. My Bills This week, I am happy to report that three more of my bills were voted out of committee and sent to the House floor. These bills address critical issues that are so important to Virginia’s families, like education and teachers, early childhood care for military families, and taking steps to combat the opioid epidemic: HB 732 [] : My bill that would require schools to stock and train staff on the administration of Naloxone, a life-saving medication, in case of an opioid overdose. This bill will help schools be prepared to recognize and respond to the tragic extent of the opioid epidemic and give our school staff the tools and training they need to save student and staff lives. This bill passed out of the House Appropriations Committee unanimously with amendments [] , 22-0, and is now on second read on the House Floor. HB 739 [] : My bill that would exempt childcare and early education programs that serve only military families and are licensed by the DOD from unnecessary and duplicative licensure in Virginia which limits the supply of child care options for our military families. Del. Tata’s HB146 [] [] was incorporated into my bill. HB 739 passed the House Floor on Friday with a vote of 96 to 0. HB731 [] : My bill that works to fix the leaky teacher pipeline and increase teacher diversity through the elimination of a duplicative, expensive, and out-of-date testing requirement for teachers. This bill will help fill teacher vacancies by ensuring this obsolete testing requirement does not push prospective teachers out of high-quality teacher preparation pathways in favor of other fields. This bill passed out of the House Education Committee with a substitute [] and a unanimous vote of, 22-0, and is now on second read on the House Floor. Subcommittees Additionally, I continue to chair three House subcommittees, and this week, two of those subcommittees met to consider the last few bills on our docket prior to crossover next week: Subcommittee on Higher Education HB690 [] : passed the bill HB719 [] : passed the bill with a substitute HB980 [] : passed the bill with a substitute HB1024 [] : passed the bill with a substitute HB1055 [] : passed the bill with a substitute HB1087 [] : passed the bill with a substitute HB1342 [] : passed the bill with a substitute and referred to the Appropriations Committee HB1357 [] : passed the bill HB1365 [] : passed the bill with amendments HB1445 [] : passed the bill with amendments HB1467 [] : passed the bill with a substitute HB211 [] : continued the bill to 2025 HB669 [] : did not pass the bill HB1008 [] : removed from the docket and will not move forward HB1051 [] : continued the bill to 2025 Subcommittee on the Department of Motor Vehicles HB425 [] : passed the bill with amendments HB812 [] : passed the bill with a substitute HB1112 [] : passed the bill with amendments HB1419 [] : passed the bill with a substitute and referred to the Appropriations Committee HB1538 [] : passed the bill with a substitute HB1213 [] : did not pass the bill This Week’s Mailbag As the session continues, so does your mail! Your ongoing engagement is so heartening, and I appreciate everyone who takes the time to write to my team about specific bills and policies that mean the most to them. This week, we received a lot of emails about two specific concerns: transportation issues and bills related to firearm safety and gun violence prevention. Thank you for reading these weekly messages, for sharing your feedback and making your voice heard. Please do not hesitate to reach out throughout the ongoing General Assembly session. Stay In Touch! As I’ve previously mentioned, crossover begins next week on February 13th. As the House of Delegates begins considering legislation coming over from the Senate, I encourage you to stay in contact with my team and continue to provide your feedback and ideas. Remember, you can email my office at DelBSewell@house.virginia.gov [DelBSewell@hous.virginia.gov] or call us at 703-261-4787. You can also drop by my Richmond office in person at 201 North 9th St., Suite 905, Richmond, VA 23219. Have a safe and enjoyable weekend, and we look forward to providing updates on how crossover is progressing and much more next week! [] Paid for by Friends of Briana Sewell. | **Republican senators block rape, incest exceptions to state's abortion ban**
The Republican-controlled Missouri Senate on Feb. 7 rejected Democratic efforts to create exceptions for cases of rape or incest to the state’s strict law banning abortion. However, the chamber failed to advance the underlying bill that seeks to prohibit Planned Parenthood clinics in the state from participating in Missouri’s Medicaid program.

Because abortion is illegal in Missouri in nearly all instances, the local Planned Parenthood clinics do not perform them and instead provide a variety of other women’s health services for which they are entitled to receive Medicaid reimbursements. However, since Planned Parenthood locations in other states do provide abortions, Republican lawmakers have unsuccessfully sought for years to keep any state funding from going to the organization.

Senate Bill 1168 would prohibit health care providers from receiving Medicaid reimbursements if they are affiliated with an out-of-state abortion provider. Democrats attempted to add amendments to the bill to allow abortions in Missouri in cases of rape or incest, but majority Republicans voted them down. After several hours of debate, the Senate couldn’t overcome a Democratic filibuster and set aside the bill without taking a final vote. However, it could return to the measure later.

The Senate debate took place one day after the launch of an initiative petition asking voters to amend the Missouri Constitution to protect reproductive rights, including abortion. Supporters must turn in signatures from a minimum of about 185,000 registered Missouri voters by early May to qualify for the Nov. 5 statewide ballot.
---
|
---
| **House approves 3 new child care tax credit programs**
The Missouri House of Representatives on Feb. 6 voted 113-39 to send legislation to the Senate creating three new tax credit programs to assist Missourians with paying for child care costs. While support for the bill was bipartisan, only Republicans voted against the bill.

One program would allow taxpayers to claim a credit equal to 75 percent of their verified child care costs. Another would grant child care providers employing three or more workers a credit equal to up to 30 percent of its employer withholding tax. The final program would allow providers to offset up to 30 percent of their capital improvement costs with credits.

According to the bill’s official fiscal estimate, the new credits would cost the state a total of $70 million a year in lost revenue. The legislation is House Bill 1488.

| **HELP FOR SMALL BUSINESS AND NON- PROFITS **
The U.S. Small Business Administration approved Missouri’s request for assistance through the federal agency’s disaster loan program. This move makes low-interest disaster loans available to small businesses and private non-profits severely impacted by the Coronavirus (COVID-19).

SBA’s Economic Injury Disaster Loans offer up to $2 million in assistance for each affected small business or non-profit. These loans can provide vital economic support to small businesses to help overcome the temporary loss of revenue they are experiencing.

Applicants will be able to apply online, receive additional disaster assistance information and [download applications](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=251be6c67c&e=a088917f89). Applicants may also call SBA’s Customer Service Center at (800) 659-2955 or email [disastercustomerservice@sba.gov](mailto:disastercustomerservice@sba.gov) for more information on SBA disaster assistance.

| **RX OUTREACH EXPANDS ELIGIBILITY **
Below is a memo from a trusted non-profit:

As a non-profit, mail-order pharmacy, Rx Outreach is uniquely positioned to help stop the spread of COVID-19 for people who are quarantined and those who may be afraid to travel outside of their homes to the clinic or pharmacy.

Rx Outreach lifted its income guidelines during this crisis so that **anyone, regardless of income, can participate in their program.**

Rx Outreach medication prices are generally 70-80% below retail and are transparent. Shipping is free.

---
|

| **LOW-COST INSULIN** Before COVID-19 insulin users were having a difficult time affording medication due to the tripling of the cost over the last decade. In the wake of COVID-19, it has become even more difficult for those who rely on insulin to afford the inflated cost. Eli Lilly and Company has introduced a program to purchase Basaglar insulin at significant cost savings. Please call Lilly Diabetes Solution Center at 833-808-1234 between 9 am and 9 pm, M-F to see if you qualify for these savings. More information can be found at the [Eli Lilly and Co. Website](https://robertsforstl.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6e3f7ee3b541518ea63c7e773&id=1f1215897c&e=a088917f89).

clean_body: Senate blocks rape, incest exceptions to state’s abortion ban | | | | | | | Capitol Report February 11, 2024 | | Sen. Steven Roberts discusses the "Revitalizing Missouri Downtowns and Main Streets Act" and what it means for the City of St. Louis. | | Contact Senator Roberts 201 W. Capitol Ave., Rm 329 Jefferson City, MO 65101 (573) 751-4415 Steve.Roberts@senate.mo.gov | | | | | | | | | | | | Republican senators block rape, incest exceptions to state's abortion ban The Republican-controlled Missouri Senate on Feb. 7 rejected Democratic efforts to create exceptions for cases of rape or incest to the state’s strict law banning abortion. However, the chamber failed to advance the underlying bill that seeks to prohibit Planned Parenthood clinics in the state from participating in Missouri’s Medicaid program. Because abortion is illegal in Missouri in nearly all instances, the local Planned Parenthood clinics do not perform them and instead provide a variety of other women’s health services for which they are entitled to receive Medicaid reimbursements. However, since Planned Parenthood locations in other states do provide abortions, Republican lawmakers have unsuccessfully sought for years to keep any state funding from going to the organization. Senate Bill 1168 would prohibit health care providers from receiving Medicaid reimbursements if they are affiliated with an out-of-state abortion provider. Democrats attempted to add amendments to the bill to allow abortions in Missouri in cases of rape or incest, but majority Republicans voted them down. After several hours of debate, the Senate couldn’t overcome a Democratic filibuster and set aside the bill without taking a final vote. However, it could return to the measure later. The Senate debate took place one day after the launch of an initiative petition asking voters to amend the Missouri Constitution to protect reproductive rights, including abortion. Supporters must turn in signatures from a minimum of about 185,000 registered Missouri voters by early May to qualify for the Nov. 5 statewide ballot. | | House approves 3 new child care tax credit programs The Missouri House of Representatives on Feb. 6 voted 113-39 to send legislation to the Senate creating three new tax credit programs to assist Missourians with paying for child care costs. While support for the bill was bipartisan, only Republicans voted against the bill. One program would allow taxpayers to claim a credit equal to 75 percent of their verified child care costs. Another would grant child care providers employing three or more workers a credit equal to up to 30 percent of its employer withholding tax. The final program would allow providers to offset up to 30 percent of their capital improvement costs with credits. According to the bill’s official fiscal estimate, the new credits would cost the state a total of $70 million a year in lost revenue. The legislation is House Bill 1488. | | 2024 SENATE BILLS \- Creates the offense of unlawful possession of a firearm by a minor \- Modifies provisions relating to homelessness \- Establishes the Revitalizing Missouri Downtowns and Main Streets Act \- Creates provisions relating to compensation for wrongful convictions \- Modifies provisions relating to facilities of historic significance \- Modifies provisions relating to school board elections in urban and metropolitan school districts \- Authorizes a sales tax for special educational services \- Authorizes a property tax for special educational services \- Modifies the calculation of weighted average daily attendance used to calculate state aid for school districts \- Creates the Missouri Geospatial Advisory Council \- Modifies provisions relating to legal representation for certain court proceedings involving children \- Modifies provisions relating to benevolent tax credits \- Establishes provisions relating to alternative dispute resolution \- Creates certificates of exemplary conduct and good moral character issued by circuit courts \- Creates provisions relating to inmate phone call fees \- Modifies provisions relating to parole eligibility \- Repeals provisions relating to the caseload of public defenders \- Modifies provisions relating to delinquent property taxes \- Modifies provisions relating to the offense of unlawful use of weapons \- Creates provisions relating to grants for nonprofit organizations at risk for terrorist attacks \- Enacts new law relating to wage range inquiries by employees and prospective employees \- Modifies the practice of dentistry to include the prescription and administration of vaccines \- Modifies eligibility for appropriations from the Waterways and Ports Trust Fund | | "Signed By Governor" | "Truly Agreed Bills" | | | | | HELP FOR SMALL BUSINESS AND NON- PROFITS The U.S. Small Business Administration approved Missouri’s request for assistance through the federal agency’s disaster loan program. This move makes low-interest disaster loans available to small businesses and private non-profits severely impacted by the Coronavirus (COVID-19). SBA’s Economic Injury Disaster Loans offer up to $2 million in assistance for each affected small business or non-profit. These loans can provide vital economic support to small businesses to help overcome the temporary loss of revenue they are experiencing. Applicants will be able to apply online, receive additional disaster assistance information and . Applicants may also call SBA’s Customer Service Center at (800) 659-2955 or email mailto:disastercustomerservice@sba.gov for more information on SBA disaster assistance. | | SAFE SHELTERING RESOURCES Sheltering in place can be difficult for anyone, and some of our neighbors might not be safe due to violence at home. If you or someone you know needs help, please visit or call 314-531-2003. You can also contact at 314-993-2777. | | COMMUNITY RESOURCE LIST The has a COVID-19 Community Economic Relief Fund. If you need help paying bills, rent, or food call 866-211-9966. : : | | | | | | MEDICAL RESOURCES The Department of Health COVID-19 hotline is open from 8 a.m. - 10 p.m. Call 314-657-1499 with questions or concerns. The Missouri Department of Health and Senior Services hotline can be reached at tel:%20\(877\%20435-8411). | | | | | | RX OUTREACH EXPANDS ELIGIBILITY Below is a memo from a trusted non-profit: As a non-profit, mail-order pharmacy, Rx Outreach is uniquely positioned to help stop the spread of COVID-19 for people who are quarantined and those who may be afraid to travel outside of their homes to the clinic or pharmacy. Rx Outreach lifted its income guidelines during this crisis so that anyone, regardless of income, can participate in their program. Rx Outreach medication prices are generally 70-80% below retail and are transparent. Shipping is free. | | LOW-COST INSULIN Before COVID-19 insulin users were having a difficult time affording medication due to the tripling of the cost over the last decade. In the wake of COVID-19, it has become even more difficult for those who rely on insulin to afford the inflated cost. Eli Lilly and Company has introduced a program to purchase Basaglar insulin at significant cost savings. Please call Lilly Diabetes Solution Center at 833-808-1234 between 9 am and 9 pm, M-F to see if you qualify for these savings. More information can be found at the . | | Check the Status of your Missouri State Tax refund | | | CITIZENS SERVICE BUREAU If you are having issues with street lights, dumpsters, trees, graffiti, potholes or other such items, please report them to the Citizens' Service Bureau. You can call 314-622-4800 or report online at | | | | | | Keep In Touch Phone: Email: mailto:Steven.Roberts@house.mo.gov Office Address: Room 329 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright © 2024 Roberts for St. Louis, All rights reserved. |LIST:DESCRIPTION| Paid for by Roberts for St. Louis, James D. David McCormick already has the support of Mitch McConnell, national Republicans, and out-of-state billionaires, but now that Charles Koch–who is worth over $50 billion–is involved, spending is going to skyrocket.

| **Meet Kevin Lincoln
**
My name is Kevin Lincoln, and my story, is the Valley’s story. I am a 3rd generation Stocktonian, raising a 4th generation in the city I love. I’m the grandson of Mexican immigrants, and my stepfather is an Army veteran.

Like many Stocktonians, my family has overcome economic setbacks. My own father struggled to survive on these streets, and was at one time, homeless. But I never let adversity define me. I joined the Marines and served in the White House Military Office, assigned to Marine One. I graduated college while working full-time and raising a family. I’ve worked in corporate America and served our community as a pastor, and as a non-profit leader.

I’m blessed with a beautiful wife, Bonnie, and two amazing children. I’ve lived a life of service, but the greatest honor of my life has been giving back to my community, through serving as Mayor of my hometown, Stockton.

subject: told you so
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
| [ ](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/61888?t=6&akid=12929%2E878179%2EfZHpWb) Guess what folks, **I was right.**

I’ve been screaming from the rooftops that my friend Josh Riley’s race here in NY-19 is the top race Democrats need to win to take back the House... and finally, folks are catching on.

In case you missed the news, Josh just earned a spot on the national Democrats’ highly exclusive ‘Red-to-Blue’ list. Only a handful of races in the entire country made the cut, and it goes to show how competitive and important this race will be.

**I hate to say ‘I told you so,’ but I kind of did.** I know Josh has what it takes to win, and I’m thrilled they see it too.

[**This is a key moment in his race, and Josh needs our help to keep up the momentum. So today, I’m asking you to split $10, $25, or whatever you can between Josh and the Voter Protection Project to help Josh win in NY-19.**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/61889?t=7&akid=12929%2E878179%2EfZHpWb) If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Josh Riley and VPP will go through immediately: | [CONTRIBUTE $10 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/61890?t=8&akid=12929%2E878179%2EfZHpWb)
---
[CONTRIBUTE $25 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/61891?t=9&akid=12929%2E878179%2EfZHpWb)
[CONTRIBUTE $50 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/61892?t=10&akid=12929%2E878179%2EfZHpWb)
[CONTRIBUTE $100 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/61893?t=11&akid=12929%2E878179%2EfZHpWb)
[Or a custom donation »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/61889?t=12&akid=12929%2E878179%2EfZHpWb)
Let’s be real: there’s no taking back the House majority without flipping districts like NY-19. I know, and national Democrats know, that Josh is just the guy to do the job.

Josh was my counsel in the Senate, and he’s the hardest working person I know. Together, we passed bills expanding rights for survivors of domestic abuse and providing new funding for addiction treatment and mental health programs. (We also made a lot of Republican witnesses look very bad.) **Josh ran in this district in 2022, and came up short by 1.6% – and he’s going to win this time with our help.**

[**So if you’re all-in to take back the House this November, I need you to get serious about Josh’s race. Please donate anything you can (seriously, every dollar helps) to the Voter Protection Project and Josh today to send Marc Molinaro packing and get the Speaker’s gavel out of Mike Johnson’s hands.**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/61889?t=13&akid=12929%2E878179%2EfZHpWb)
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Josh Riley and VPP will go through immediately:

| | Peter,

Running a campaign in California is expensive — and the primary for California’s Senate seat might be the most expensive Senate primary we’ve seen in U.S. history.

clean_body: | | If you want to help The Collective PAC build Black political power up and down the ballot, and across the country, then you’re in the right place. If you'd like to unsubscribe | | Peter, Running a campaign in California is expensive — and the primary for California’s Senate seat might be the most expensive Senate primary we’ve seen in U.S. history. Barbara Lee’s opponents are sitting on millions, Peter. With Super Tuesday just a few weeks away, we need your help to make sure she has what it takes to come out on top. | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately to The Collective PAC. | | | | | | If we fall short, we might miss our chance to elect another Black woman to the Senate in 2024. Barbara Lee has spent over 20 years fighting for the state of California and our country in Congress. At a time when we need bold solutions in the Senate to tackle the challenges we face, we know Barbara can be the one to deliver them. But it’s going to take serious resources — she’s up against opponents who have millions in the bank. If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately to The Collective PAC. | | | | | | Thank you, The Collective PAC The Collective PAC is strategically focused on creating a truly equitable democracy where our nation’s local, state and federal governments have diverse and talented elected leadership and Black people are fully represented in positions of power to create the policies necessary to progress our communities forward. Since our inception in 2016, we have helped 110 candidates win general elections at the local, state and federal levels. We strive to support the next wave of progressive and unapologetic Black candidates who will usher in a sea of change in our political system while helping America fulfill its promise of democracy by creating a government that will truly be for the people and of the people. Hey Derek — Folks might be tuning into the actual Super Bowl tonight, but our finance team is fired up about our own version of the Super Bowl — **our pre-primary fundraising deadline, which is coming up in 3 days.**
On February 14th, we close the books on one of our last fundraising reporting periods before the March 5th primary.
This will be one of our last chances to show everyone the grassroots momentum behind Adam’s campaign.
Over the last year, this team has supercharged Adam’s campaign with grassroots contributions.
With each deadline, you’ve stepped up in a big way to support Adam as he runs to take his fight for our democracy and an economy that works for everyone to the U.S. Senate.

name: The GIFFORDS Team
subject: 🏆 Announcing our GIFFORDS Champion Award winners 🏆
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
We’re so proud to have these four leaders by our side as we enter the next decade of our movement's lifesaving work
#

Friends,
We’re so excited to share the four inaugural recipients of the [**GIFFORDS Champion Award**](https://go.giffords.org/go/51245?t=1&akid=7623%2E3935704%2EUKPMkB)! The Champion Award was created to bring new voices into the fight for stronger gun laws and celebrate a new wave of gun safety champions.
[](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-feb24.CYoP11&refcode=em240211-1-30-nor&t=2&akid=7623%2E3935704%2EUKPMkB)_“I know what it’s like to sit in fear inside of a classroom both as a student and as a substitute teacher. Our children, and all of our communities, deserve to feel safe. Now, the next generation of leaders is here to ensure that our nation moves towards a safer, more inclusive future rather than choosing to stay trapped in a cycle of violence and pain.”_
**—Averie Bishop, Texas House Candidate (HD-112)**
* * *
[](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-feb24.CYoP11&refcode=em240211-1-30-nor&t=3&akid=7623%2E3935704%2EUKPMkB)_“GIFFORDS has been fearless and effective in the fight to end gun violence in America and their work is moving us to a future that is safer and brighter for the next generation. I am honored to have earned this distinction and I am ready to get to work in Congress to champion the critical work of ending gun violence in our communities.”_
**—Lateefah Simon, US House Candidate (CA-12)**
* * *
[](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-feb24.CYoP11&refcode=em240211-1-30-nor&t=4&akid=7623%2E3935704%2EUKPMkB)_“As a veteran and trauma surgeon, I have spent my career dedicated to keeping our communities safe and healthy. I look forward to working alongside GIFFORDS to pass universal background checks, raise the minimum age to purchase a firearm, and reinstate the assault weapons ban as a member of Congress.”_
**—Brian Williams, US House Candidate (TX-32)**
* * *
[](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-feb24.CYoP11&refcode=em240211-1-30-nor&t=5&akid=7623%2E3935704%2EUKPMkB)_”As a Latina, a mom, and a former public school teacher, I know all too well the daily toll that gun violence has taken on our communities. I am running for New York State Senate to move us forward so that our kids don’t have to worry about school shootings. I’m running so that our neighbors are safe in their places of worship, our grocery stores, everywhere.”_
**-- Yvette Valdés Smith, New York Senate Candidate (SD-39)**
* * *
Averie, Lateefah, Brian, and Yvette represent a new wave of leaders who are running up and down the ballot with a dedication to ending gun violence. We’re so proud to have them by our side as we enter the next decade of our movement’s lifesaving work.
[**Will you make a contribution today to help us continue our work uplifting and electing gun safety champions who will put the safety of our communities first?**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-feb24.CYoP11&refcode=em240211-1-30-nor&t=6&akid=7623%2E3935704%2EUKPMkB)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_
| | **[CONTRIBUTE $3](https://go.giffords.org/go/17015?c0=giffords-em-feb24.xFye0V&amount=3&refcode=em240211-1-30-nor&t=7&akid=7623%2E3935704%2EUKPMkB)**
---
****
| **[CONTRIBUTE OTHER](https://go.giffords.org/go/17015?c0=giffords-em-feb24.xFye0V&refcode=em240211-1-30-nor&t=8&akid=7623%2E3935704%2EUKPMkB)**
---
****
Thank you,
The team at GIFFORDS

Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. www.Giffords.org
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com . We try to send only the most important information and opportunities to participate via email, but click [here](https://go.giffords.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=9&akid=7623%2E3935704%2EUKPMkB) if you'd like to unsubscribe. Giffords PAC was started to support elected officials who step up to fight the gun violence epidemic. Thank you for standing with Gabby and the Giffords team.

name: Sherrod Brown (via Mazie Hirono)
subject: An uphill climb
body:
|
#
_Hi Peter, this is Mazie. I need to share this important message from my dear friend and colleague, Sherrod Brown. He is facing a fiercely competitive re-election campaign in Ohio, and if we’re going to protect our razor-thin majority, he needs to win in November. Please split a donation between our campaigns if you’re able today to keep Sherrod and his pro-worker agenda in the Senate._
* * *

body:
The Senate Majority is Up to You

|
---
Friend,

We're at a difficult spot in this primary fundraising push... [**And I need your help.**](http://links.banksforsenate.com/u/click?_t=588630feb05642bf856633600a62821b&_m=7849b779056543e8806977b643606461&_e=gmldVW192BkhrdZY9gL1xllttl2XZIsNqGcljV_MD6wJ_a0mOkvAzAD6E621gT6JGyOxTU5wIpshu3FDY5Eum9MSJ4pnUGeAxD_ms0-2ECe5-le-yESdNL3xizufkeovzRTVPwtSiU0dy0KjPh-06oc-z3t8zOL468-grj25crN3gT29_Q9aQ1V1Y_8rI6NJfpVeimrcWQrmItqpYbVI8vDgpDWVEmYkCdryq8APgRBEeZJ8O-pNPjov_EW4WKW0jYJP7j5B-Y6yxY-hxHxzldWPmtlbLs1Q2jVaorZr6q1kowLfqbfcP7Qr5l248xOmoGJxDqZwNjXd7dBMpmAi0A%3D%3D)
The Senate Majority hangs in the balance this November, and we need all hands on deck to be prepared.

At the same time, I have a lifelong Democrat voter running against me in this primary. **A candidate who is backed by George Soros in his run against me.**

Indiana deserves better.

Indiana deserves honesty.

Indiana deserves an America First Fighter in the Senate.

So, my team just told me that we have been authorized to [**MATCH all donations**](http://links.banksforsenate.com/u/click?_t=588630feb05642bf856633600a62821b&_m=7849b779056543e8806977b643606461&_e=gmldVW192BkhrdZY9gL1xllttl2XZIsNqGcljV_MD6wJ_a0mOkvAzAD6E621gT6JGyOxTU5wIpshu3FDY5Eum9MSJ4pnUGeAxD_ms0-2ECe5-le-yESdNL3xizufkeovzRTVPwtSiU0dy0KjPh-06oc-z3t8zOL468-grj25crP22x_AJPFOQ3toEJa8-HQgBkPJnLcA9liKCVtb-ghuQxkc3zfUBHBVIDjc5kHx6kzi5jLULo9lI5RGOBaQfBJH3PNv29XoA2xM5Ki5i-pyCj7ZJlMVWrZJYlUVM_IdEGWC7FNPkc_KdMcIj7sCj8_VEi67hdFPXcDQ05on7Gpy3A%3D%3D) that come in the door [**until MIDNIGHT ONLY.**](http://links.banksforsenate.com/u/click?_t=588630feb05642bf856633600a62821b&_m=7849b779056543e8806977b643606461&_e=gmldVW192BkhrdZY9gL1xllttl2XZIsNqGcljV_MD6wJ_a0mOkvAzAD6E621gT6JGyOxTU5wIpshu3FDY5Eum9MSJ4pnUGeAxD_ms0-2ECe5-le-yESdNL3xizufkeovzRTVPwtSiU0dy0KjPh-06oc-z3t8zOL468-grj25crMe4K9f-8acwCffYzIZJLzSSFv1BCWme6aPO31dZY9y38Kre5PHMRIxHBA8o4eflLk_uWPQ3x2JymwGPRV-TwzpTkA64fh6QHBh0YATCOWxJi7Arms5CI1yuWVToYuHnW4LuJMVdf1U5xwpjKGhOOO8OXLQC9sjaBOOgTzlGR92sg%3D%3D)

[**_You could have double the impact on the future of Indiana and America if you choose to pitch in here._**](http://links.banksforsenate.com/u/click?_t=588630feb05642bf856633600a62821b&_m=7849b779056543e8806977b643606461&_e=gmldVW192BkhrdZY9gL1xllttl2XZIsNqGcljV_MD6wJ_a0mOkvAzAD6E621gT6JGyOxTU5wIpshu3FDY5Eum9MSJ4pnUGeAxD_ms0-2ECe5-le-yESdNL3xizufkeovzRTVPwtSiU0dy0KjPh-06oc-z3t8zOL468-grj25crP_c0fuczvmOF4uz6gSajzvgzyOImoHGHLP4H-SIE3_j4F0kGbMET3fnk2vTHCtF3P2B8s0_At7uBAOcUjqPxJvadbRf5YFj7yLwxptpqo5wT_CDR0mh4uDKf9Lx6y7gELNkXveXrINewjxPFXtP4KCGsv31uIx7D3yCd8W0yFBAg%3D%3D)
A Senate Majority controlled by the Far Left could lead to the further desecration of conservative ideals and America First leadership we have fought so hard for.

name: THANK YOU JAMIE RASKIN! (via VPP)
subject: Sign the letter before your signature spot closes >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
He just DESTROYED the GOP over their impeachment plot:
| [ ](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/61950?t=6&akid=12953%2E878179%2ENBaBjW) Jamie Raskin is a HERO! He’s one of the TOP Democrats fighting to end the MAGA mob’s impeachment scheme — and he’s not backing down.

[**The GOP is hell-bent on SILENCING Raskin, so we need a SURGE of support from folks like you to show we have his back! HURRY: ADD YOUR NAME BY MIDNIGHT to thank Rep. Jamie Raskin »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/61951?t=7&akid=12953%2E878179%2ENBaBjW) | [YOUR ONLY CHANCE TO SIGN: CHIP IN $3 TO ADD YOUR NAME NOW »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/61951?t=8&akid=12953%2E878179%2ENBaBjW)
---
TO: Rep. Jamie Raskin
FROM: A proud Democrat
Subject: You’re a hero! **_To Rep. Jamie Raskin — thank you for being one of the top defenders of our democracy!_**

We owe _so much_ of our Democratic success this year to you.

You were a crucial figure in averting the disastrous government shutdown (twice!). You called out the GOP for electing extremist Speaker Mike Johnson.

**And now, you’re fighting tooth and nail to save President Biden from a baseless impeachment — even in the face of vile MAGA attacks.**

[**I know Republicans are using every dirty trick in the book to SILENCE you — So today, I’m fighting back and chipping in $3 to the Voter Protection Project to elect more amazing Democrats like you who will flip the House and save our democracy! »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/61951?t=9&akid=12953%2E878179%2ENBaBjW) | [Donate $3 to sign now »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/61950?t=10&akid=12953%2E878179%2ENBaBjW)
---
**_X A proud Democrat_**
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=12953%2E878179%2ENBaBjW). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=12953%2E878179%2ENBaBjW).**

name: ami
subject: 24 hours left to take action
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bera for Congress
body:
| Peter, next week will be the six year anniversary since the devastating Parkland shooting. Despite progress for common sense gun reform, there is still no federal ban on assault weapons.

Weapons of war do not belong in our communities, Peter. Assault weapons are designed to wreak havoc and that’s why nearly a dozen states banned them.

My Democratic colleagues and I have been hard at work trying to address this crisis, but the gun lobby has the GOP at their beck and call, blocking any efforts for an assault weapons ban or further gun violence prevention legislation.

**_[Join me in calling to reinstate the assault weapons ban and sign my petition before the deadline at midnight.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/78830757/454594044/-872666029?source=EM_NBI_AB_20240211_PT_1.3_3MO_candidate_NA_NA_gvp&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS85MDU3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MWFjYmY5OC0yZWM5LWVlMTEtODVmOS0wMDIyNDgyMjM3OTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8Hiuh_ZFJ_nzs8SjysPHiA43ntjkGt449ImqqtJN-kE=&emci=4facbf98-2ec9-ee11-85f9-002248223794&emdi=51acbf98-2ec9-ee11-85f9-002248223794&ceid=32394541)_**
